title: Helictochloa pruinosa
intro: Helictochloa pruinosa, also known as alpine hair grass, is a perennial plant species belonging to the grass family (Poaceae). Traditionally, Helictochloa pruinosa has been used in herbal medicine to support respiratory health, with potential benefits including relief from coughs and colds, as well as anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Helictochloa pruinosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Alpine hair grass is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into cough syrups and respiratory health supplements due to its soothing and expectorant effects.
taxonomy: Helictochloa pruinosa is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is a member of the subfamily Pooideae and is closely related to other species within this group. Helictochloa pruinosa is also known to be a part of the tribe Poeae, which comprises various types of grasses and wildflowers. Further classification of this plant places it within the subtribe Airinae, indicating its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships. This classification helps scientists understand the plant's origins and behavior.
